metadata.name: Alpine Ski Slopes
metadata.description: 60 questions about the ski slopes of Alpine Meadows and Squaw Valley (now Palisades Tahoe) — covering geography, history, culture, art, science, and racing.
metadata.author: Faiser

questions.questionText: In which US state are both Alpine Meadows and Squaw Valley ski resorts located?
What large alpine lake lies just a few miles east of Palisades Tahoe?
What mountain range contains both Alpine Meadows and Squaw Valley?
What small town on the north shore of Lake Tahoe sits just a few miles from Palisades Tahoe?
What railroad town roughly 12 miles northwest of Lake Tahoe serves as a gateway city to Palisades Tahoe?
The valley in which Squaw Valley sits is drained by which creek that flows into the Truckee River?
Approximately how many acres of skiable terrain does Palisades Tahoe (both villages combined) offer?
What is the base elevation in feet at the Alpine Meadows village?
At roughly what elevation in feet does the summit of KT-22 chair at Palisades Tahoe sit?
Which Nevada county border is Lake Tahoe's eastern shoreline closest to, making part of the lake lie in Nevada?
Which 1993 snowboard comedy film features the character Charles 'Chunk' Godfrey and was largely set at a ski resort?
What iconic 1998 snowboard trick competition at the Winter X Games made halfpipe a mainstream spectator sport?
Which legendary American skier nicknamed 'The Herminator' dominated World Cup racing in the late 1990s?
Which American snowboarder won three Olympic halfpipe gold medals and trained extensively at Lake Tahoe resorts?
What 1969 ski film starring Robert Redford was filmed partly in the Sierra Nevada and helped romanticize downhill racing?
Which Tahoe-area skier won back-to-back Olympic downhill golds in 2010 and 2014?
The 1960 Winter Olympics opening ceremony at Squaw Valley was narrated by which famous Disney figure?
Which ski film production company, known for films like 'Superheroes of Stoke,' frequently featured Palisades Tahoe riders in the 2010s?
Squaw Valley's 1960 Olympics were the first Winter Games to be broadcast live on television in the US — on which network?
Which Squaw Valley native and freestyle skier won the first-ever Olympic moguls gold medal in 1992 for the US?
In what year did Squaw Valley host the Winter Olympic Games?
What is the new combined name of Squaw Valley and Alpine Meadows since their 2021 rebranding?
In what decade was Alpine Meadows ski resort originally founded?
In what year did Alpine Meadows open for its first ski season?
What devastating natural disaster struck Alpine Meadows on March 31, 1982, killing seven people?
Which developer and entrepreneur originally built and opened Squaw Valley ski resort in 1949?
What company owns and operates Palisades Tahoe today, having acquired Squaw Valley Alpine Meadows in 2021?
The 1960 Squaw Valley Olympics were awarded to the resort over which other favored candidate city?
Who was the ski patrol dog whose handler, Frank Yeatman, survived the 1982 Alpine Meadows avalanche — the dog that helped locate a survivor named Anna Conrad?
In what year did KSL Capital Partners acquire Squaw Valley Ski Holdings, preceding the eventual Alterra merger?
What iconic style of illustrated resort map, showing a bird's-eye perspective of the mountain with labeled runs, is called a what?
What color symbol is used on North American ski trail signs to mark an intermediate run?
What shape and color symbol marks the easiest beginner runs on a North American ski trail map?
Which legendary ski poster artist, known for his vibrant Art Deco mountain prints, created iconic imagery for resorts like Sun Valley in the mid-20th century?
The official Palisades Tahoe trail map uses an oblique aerial illustration style. What term describes this artistic bird's-eye mountain perspective technique?
What double black diamond symbol shape is used on North American ski maps to mark the most challenging expert-only terrain?
Palisades Tahoe's logo features a stylized mountain peak. What two colors dominate the resort's current branding?
Which Swiss graphic designer is widely credited with defining the clean, modernist aesthetic of mid-century European ski resort posters?
The famous illustrated trail maps of many Western ski resorts were produced for decades by which artist collective based in Colorado?
What photographic technique — using a long focal length lens to compress the apparent distance between ski runs — is commonly used in resort promotional photography?
What type of mechanical lift carries skiers up a mountain while they sit in chairs hanging from a moving cable?
What is the name of the crystalline solid water structure that forms naturally as snowflakes?
What is the term for artificially produced snow blown onto ski slopes using machines?
What explosive device is commonly used by ski patrol to trigger controlled avalanche releases before the slopes open?
What is the name of the system installed on ski boots and bindings that releases the boot during a fall to prevent leg fractures?
What type of high-speed enclosed gondola system connects the two Palisades Tahoe base areas — replacing the need to drive between resorts?
Snowmaking guns mix compressed air with water to produce snow. What minimum temperature in Fahrenheit is typically required for snowmaking to work?
What is the term for the specific type of persistent weak layer in a snowpack — a faceted crystal layer — that creates high avalanche danger days after a storm?
Palisades Tahoe uses a remote avalanche control system called Gazex. What propellant gas mixture is detonated in Gazex exploders to trigger avalanche releases?
What is the scientific term for the density measurement of snow — grams per cubic centimeter — that ski patrol uses to assess avalanche potential?
What is the famous expert chairlift and run at Palisades Tahoe known for steep, ungroomed terrain — abbreviated KT?
What symbol on a trail sign indicates an expert-only, most difficult ski run?
What is the name of the international ski racing organization that sanctions World Cup events?
What is the name of the very steep, narrow expert chute at Alpine Meadows known for its intimidating pitch and tight walls?
KT-22 at Palisades Tahoe is named for how many kick turns a pioneer skier needed to descend the face. How many kick turns gave the run its name?
What is the name of the terrain park at Palisades Tahoe's main mountain that features halfpipes, rails, and jump lines for freestyle skiers and snowboarders?
Alpine Meadows was home to a prominent women's World Cup downhill race in the 1970s and 80s. What is the name of the main race venue run?
Which American ski racer, a Lake Tahoe local, won a World Cup downhill on the KT-22 course in 1969 — one of the first American men to win a downhill?
What is the vertical drop in feet of the main Palisades (former Squaw Valley) mountain from summit to base?
In competitive ski racing, what is the name of the discipline combining a downhill and giant slalom section into a single run, which was debuted at the 1960 Squaw Valley Olympics?
